description: We unite long-term strategy with best-practice project delivery, transforming UK major projects and programmes. NISTA works with the Cabinet Office and HM Treasury .

details.body:
We unite long-term strategy with best-practice project delivery, transforming UK major projects and programmes.

NISTA works with the Cabinet Office and HM Treasury.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Workforce demand data central to next Pipeline, giving investors and industry a clearer view to support long-term planning for skills and investment
ordered_featured_documents.title: Infrastructure Pipeline update signals future workforce needs

image.alt_text: Image of a newly constructed prison

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New GMPP offers more targeted support from the centre of government for the most nationally significant, highest-impact projects and programmes
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government refocuses major projects to boost delivery of national priorities

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New Chair brings extensive experience in infrastructure investment and delivery to leadership of expert body advising on NISTA’s strategy and objectives.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Julia Prescot CBE appointed Chair of NISTA Council

ordered_featured_documents.summary: 10 Year Infrastructure Strategy published today (19 June) will deliver on the Government’s growth mission, as part of the Plan for Change, transforming how infrastructure projects are planned and delivered.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Decade long Infrastructure Strategy to deliver stability, investment and national renewal

available_translations.title: National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority

role_appointments.title: Becky Wood - Head of Project Delivery Function

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer leads the National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority, which reports jointly to the Chancellor of the Exchequer.

This includes overseeing the National Infrastructure and Construction Pipeline, which contains projections of £600bn of public and private infrastructure investment, and supporting the Government’s Major Projects Portfolio, which contains around 150 of the Government’s most complex and high-risk projects. The Chief Executive Officer also leads the professionalisation of the Project Delivery community in Government.

role_appointments.title: Becky Wood - Interim Chief Executive Officer

details.body:
Leads the economics and analysis team within the National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority (NISTA), which provides economic and analytical advice on infrastructure to senior officials and ministers across NISTA, HMT and relevant infrastructure departments.

In addition to providing robust and well-evidenced economic and analytical advice to support the 10 year Infrastructure Strategy, the Chief Economic supports the development and use of high quality economic and analytical expertise across NISTA; ensures NISTA policies and decisions are informed by rigorous evidence and constitute value for money; works across Whitehall to ensure robust and effective economic analysis of infrastructure feeds into government decision making; and contributes with other chief economists to a vibrant Government Economic Service.

role_appointments.title: Karina Singh - Head of Project Delivery Profession

details.body:
In this role, Fiona leads the Function, Profession & Standards team, responsible for building project delivery capability in government and creating a world-class project delivery profession.

This includes setting standards for how government delivers projects; developing career pathways and professional accreditation; growing and managing talent; and working to increase diversity and inclusion across the profession. The team also runs the project delivery fast stream and apprenticeship schemes, and development and delivery of project delivery leadership and learning programmes, including the world class Major Projects Leadership Academy.

role_appointments.title: Karina Singh - Director of Function, Profession & Standards

details.body:
Leads the Function, Insights & Professions team within NISTA, responsible for building project delivery capability in government and creating a world-class project delivery profession.

This includes setting standards for how government delivers projects; developing career pathways and professional accreditation; growing and managing talent; and working to increase diversity and inclusion across the profession. The team also runs the project delivery fast stream and apprenticeship schemes, and development and delivery of project delivery leadership and learning programmes, including the world class Major Projects Leadership Academy.

role_appointments.title: Karina Singh - Director of Function, Insights & Professions

details.body: The Director of Transformation is responsible for the successful design, implementation, integration, and delivery of transformation programmes resulting from our business strategy.

In Jon’s current role as Director of Infrastructure, Enterprise and Growth he is responsible for setting the overall strategic direction and leadership for the delivery of the Government’s critical projects and programmes, for the departments within the Infrastructure, Enterprise and Growth sector that include: BEIS, DIT, FCO, DfID, DfT, MHCLG, DCMS, DEFRA.

Jon leads a team of Project Delivery Advisers with deep project delivery and technical expertise who work across departments to support their major projects. This team works with departments to focus the input from the IPA to ensure projects are set up for success and to drive improved project delivery.

details.body: Providing policy advice on infrastructure needs and leading the NISTA strategy and policy function, covering economic infrastructure - digital & telecoms, energy, flood risk management, transport, water and wastewater, and waste. The team provides long-term policy advice to ministers supported by robust analysis, modelling and extensive engagement with the sectors, regulators and other experts.

details.body:
In Alison’s current role as Director of Public and Security Services she is responsible for setting the overall strategic direction and leadership for the delivery of the Government’s critical projects and programmes, for the departments within the Public and Security Services sector that include MoD, MoJ, HO, DHSC, DfE, HMRC, DWP, NCA, ONS and CO.

Alison leads a team providing deep project delivery and technical expertise to departments on the Government’s Major Projects Portfolio to ensure projects are set up for success and to drive improved project delivery.

details.body:
The Deputy Chief Executive Officer deputises for the IPA’s CEO as required in addition to managing the IPA’s Finance and International teams. In this role Matthew’s team supports HMT and IPA teams when they are setting policy for the use of private finance to deliver investment in public infrastructure, assets and services. This includes responsibility for Public Private Partnerships (PPPs) and the UK Guarantees Scheme (UKGS).

The Head of Project Finance leads the project finance profession in government and is a spokesperson for the IPA and government on the use of private finance to deliver public projects.

details.body:
The Interim Chief Executive leads the Infrastructure and Projects Authority, which reports jointly to the Chancellor of the Exchequer and the Minister for the Cabinet Office.

This includes overseeing the National Infrastructure and Construction Pipeline, which contains projections of £600bn of public and private infrastructure investment, and supporting the Government’s Major Projects Portfolio, which contains around 150 of the Government’s most complex and high risk projects. The Interim Chief Executive also leads the professionalisation of the Project Delivery and Project Finance communities in Government.

details.body: Managing the team that ensures NISTA is set up and operates in a way that enables it to deliver key ministerial functions. They are responsible for leading the change programme, reporting directly to the NISTA Chief Executive Officer. They are responsible for overseeing the change programme, including setting out a clear articulation of the NISTA vision and objectives; developing a Target Operating Model to deliver NISTA’s functions; defining governance processes including supporting the Council; working with key external stakeholders; and ensuring staff are fully engaged and consulted in the Change programme.

role_appointments.title: Jen Coe / Hannah Brown - Change Team Leader

ordered_roles.title: Director of Public and Security Services

details.body:
Leads a team of Commercial Advisers within NISTA with deep professional knowledge and experience in specialist fields. They provide expert advice to HM Treasury, Cabinet Office and government Departments to improve the performance of project delivery in Infrastructure, Enterprise & Growth and Public & Security Services.

Current NISTA responsibilities include leadership of the Transforming Infrastructure Delivery programme, advice to the Palace of Westminster restoration and renewal project, Oxford - Cambridge corridor development, Building Safety programme, HS2 stations development and construction industry reform.

Responsible for setting the overall strategic direction and leadership for the delivery of the Government’s critical projects and programmes, for the departments within the Infrastructure, Enterprise and Growth sector that include: BEIS, DIT, FCO, DfID, DfT, MHCLG, DCMS, DEFRA.
